data.current_description:
Get an overview of time entries.



HoorayHR Time Tracking explained



HoorayHR Time Tracking works by dividing time into entries. Each time entry spans a single day or a week (Mon-Sun). The time span of an entry is determined by the time tracking policy of the user.

Each entry has a list of activities. Activities are like work periods or breaks. Activities cannot overlap, but one can start at the same time as the previous one ends to create a continuous work period.

Activities of type `WORK` can have labels and notes attached, while activities of type `BREAK` cannot.

Time entries are created automatically based on the provided dates of the activities. It is only possible to create activities and entries within active time tracking policies for users.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve time entries with activities and details
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of time entries for specified users and date ranges. Time entries contain activities (work periods or breaks) that span either a single day or a full week (Mon-Sun), determined by the user's time tracking policy. Supports filtering by user, date range, and other parameters. Returns time entry objects with associated activities, labels, and notes. Activities of type WORK can include labels and notes; BREAK activities cannot.

data.current_description:
Get an overview of time entries.



HoorayHR Time Tracking explained



HoorayHR Time Tracking works by dividing time into entries. Each time entry spans a single day or a week (Mon-Sun). The time span of an entry is determined by the time tracking policy of the user.

Each entry has a list of activities. Activities are like work periods or breaks. Activities cannot overlap, but one can start at the same time as the previous one ends to create a continuous work period.

Activities of type `WORK` can have labels and notes attached, while activities of type `BREAK` cannot.

Time entries are created automatically based on the provided dates of the activities. It is only possible to create activities and entries within active time tracking policies for users.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve time entries with activities
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of time entries for specified users and date ranges. Each time entry contains activities (work periods or breaks) based on the user's time tracking policy. Supports filtering by user ID, date range, and other parameters. Returns time entry objects with associated activities, labels, and notes. Activities of type WORK can include labels and notes; BREAK activities cannot.
